title: Blind Transfers
description: Build confidential transfer_to_blind, blind_transfer and transfer_from_blind operations
advancedNotice: This is an advanced tool. This wallet cannot generate commitments, range proofs or blinding factors — you must supply the cryptographic values yourself. The completed operation is exported for an external wallet to sign and broadcast.
toBlindDescription: Move funds from a transparent balance into a blind commitment.
fromBlindDescription: Move funds from a blind commitment back to a transparent account.
amountInfo: The quantity of the selected asset to move. Enter it in the asset's normal units (e.g. 1.5 BTS); it is converted to blockchain precision automatically and cannot exceed your available balance.
assetInfo: The asset being transferred. Only assets that permit confidential transfers are listed. Pick this first — the amount field unlocks once an asset is selected.
blindingFactorInfo: A secret 32-byte random value (as hex) used to hide the amount inside a Pedersen commitment. It is generated by your confidential-transfer tooling; the receiver needs it (via the memo) to later spend the funds. Keep it secret.
toInfo: The transparent account that will receive the de-blinded funds. Search and select an existing on-chain account.
commitmentInfo: A 33-byte hex Pedersen commitment that cryptographically hides the amount while proving it is valid. It encodes (amount + blinding factor) and is produced by your confidential-transfer tooling — paste the exact hex value here.
rangeProofInfo: A hex zero-knowledge proof that the committed amount is non-negative (within a valid range) without revealing it. Prevents hidden negative/overflow amounts. Generated alongside the commitment — paste the full hex string.
weightThresholdInfo: The total signature weight required to spend this blind output. Must be met by the sum of the weights of the signing keys/accounts below (e.g. threshold 1 with one key of weight 1 = a single required signer).
accountAuthsInfo: On-chain accounts allowed to control this output, each with a weight. Their combined weight (with key authorities) must reach the threshold to spend.
keyAuthsInfo: Public keys (BTS...) allowed to control this output, each with a weight. Use a blind account's public key here to make it the recipient.
searchAccountDescription: Search for the account to use.
